titleStmt.title: SII 22.37: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 37. (A.R. No. 37 of 1906) TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR. AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL. TRIBH. KULŌTTUNGA-CHŌḶA III—9th YEAR (A.D. 1186-7)

summary.p: The king is given the epithet ‘who was pleased to take Madurai’. This records yet another gift of the same kind by the reclamation of 700 kuḻi of land each at the dēvadana village Erukkāṭṭuchchēri by two persons, one by name Sattivananāyakaṉ alias Dānavinōda-Pallavaraiyaṉ of Mūlaṅguḍi in Nallāṟṟūr-nāḍu a division of Virudarājabhayankara-vaḷanāḍu and the other called Bārantāṅgināṉ alias Kalikaḍindaśōḻa-Pallavaraiyaṉ of Magadaiyūr.
Below this inscription is an unfinished single line record of the [2]6th year of Tribh. Rājarājadēva stopping with the mention of the dēvakanmis of the Kālakāladēver temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaikoṇṭaruḷiṉa śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu Oṉpatāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa vaḷa nāṭṭu Ākkūrnāṭṭu tirukkaṭavūr Uṭaiyār śrīkālakālatevar
koyilil tirununtāviḷakku Eriya virutarājapayaṅkaravaḷanāṭṭu nallāṟṟūr nāṭṭu mulaṅkuṭiyil kāṇiyuṭaiya Aṇaikkuṭaiyān cattivananāyakanāna tāna vinotappallavaraiyaṉ Ittevar tevatāṉam
Erukkāṭṭucceriyil maṇaṟkuṉṟil viḷainilamākak kallina kuḻi 7 100 Eḻunūṟṟukkum nāḷ Oṉṟukku ney Uḻakkāka Onpatāvatu Āṉimātamutal cantirātittavaraiyum Erikkaḥ makataiyūruṭaiyāṉ paārantāṅkināṉāṉa kalikaṭinta coḻap
pallavaraiyaṉ vaitta tirununtāviḷakku 1 Oṉṟukkum Ittevar teva tāṉam Erukkāṭṭucceriyil maṇaṟkuṉṟil viḷainilamākak kallina kuḻi 7 100 Eḻunūṟ ṟukkum nāḷ Oṉṟukku ney Uḻakkāka cantirātittavaraiyum Erikkaḥ

svasti śrī . tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī Irājarājatevarkku yāṇṭu 26 jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu Ākkūrnāṭṭu Ampaṉāṭu Uḷpaṭameviya tirukkaṭavūr Uṭaiyār śrī kālakālatevar koyil Āticaṇṭeśvara tevarkaṉ mikaḷukku IStops here.
Digital edition of SII 22.37 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
